extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping vomiting from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. vomiting appears only in r8 [priority 100]: IF (back_pain and pallor and chest_pain and vision_loss and severe_headache and vomiting) THEN acuity := self_care. self_care is the lowest acuity, and priority 100 dominates every other rule.

Counterexample (the vomiting=true input given): back_pain=T, pallor=T, chest_pain=T, vision_loss=T, severe_headache=T, vomiting=T, abdominal_pain=F, rash=F.

Compare the pair required by the property:
- BEFORE (same input but vomiting=F): r8 does not fire (it needs vomiting). Matching rules include r1 (pallor -> emergent, pri 3) and r5 (vision_loss -> emergent, pri 3), plus several urgent rules at pri 2. Highest priority wins -> acuity = emergent.
- AFTER (vomiting=T): all six conditions of r8 are satisfied, so r8 fires at priority 100, beating r1/r5 (pri 3). acuity = self_care.

Setting vomiting true drops acuity from emergent to self_care, which is strictly lower in the listed order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent]. This violates monotonicity. The high-priority self_care override rule r8 is the defect.
